svg.aria-label: Two panels. Part A, generation: three sources - the codebase in brownfield, the specs in greenfield, and the human at intake - each derive specific artifacts. The codebase analysis produces the Inventory Report, whose module map gives one dev agent per module, whose conventions give the three project-specific rules, whose risky operations give the deny list and the hooks, and whose glob sets give the path frontmatter that makes rules lazy-load. The specs give FR clusters, one dev agent per feature domain, and the db, ui and ai flags. The human answers only what code cannot decide. All three converge into vars.json, which drives scaffold.py, a deterministic funnel that copies real files, substitutes variables, resolves flag blocks, never overwrites, and exits non-zero on an unresolved variable. It emits the agents, the rules, the commands, the hooks with settings.json, and the task board. The model authors only what cannot be templated. Part B, mutual constraint: a green enforcement band of hooks, deny list and maxTurns encloses a closed ring in which the orchestrator dispatches but cannot build, spec-guardian locks the contract, the dev agents write code but only inside their module paths, the reviewers gate them with no Edit and no Write, and the task board holds the orchestrator to account. Governance decides which model may touch a class of data before dispatch, and the rules are drawn soft and dashed because they are advisory.
svg.font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
svg.title: How the harness is built, and how it holds itself
svg.text: How the harness is built, and how it holds itself
Part A - generation: nothing is invented. Part B - constraint: nothing is trusted.
PART A GENERATION - where every generated file comes from
Every generated thing traces to the codebase, to the specs, or to an answer you gave.
codebase analysis, read-only, produces the Inventory Report you confirm
-> one dev agent per module, scoped to real paths
-> the settings.json deny list, and which hooks
-> the paths: frontmatter, so rules lazy-load
-> one dev agent per feature domain
answers only what the code cannot decide:
commit identity, dev OS (flag windows or posix)
ui, db, ai, audit, and exactly one
the one funnel every file passes
The model authors only what cannot be templated: the routing table, each dev agent's scope,
and the 3 project-specific rules. Everything else is a file copy.
PART B MUTUAL CONSTRAINT - each one is held by another
Not a hierarchy. A closed loop, inside a band none of them can open.
HARD - shell scripts and glob rules. No model gets a vote, not even the orchestrator.
what no agent can even see
gate the dev agents, and have
NO Edit, NO Write. A reviewer
No agent is trusted, including the one doing the routing. Each is bounded by something it does not control.
